source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/92_인증 - FDA/Attachment/Test report/033_MD RF EMC test report (Rev).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: KES Co., Ltd. FCC test report KES-RF240918-R1 for H&abyz Portable X-Ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents FCC Part 15 radiated restricted band/emission and antenna requirement results for the tested sample; the report states it cannot be used for certification.
keyPoints: Product item is Portable X-Ray System with model HnX-P1 and variant model HnX-PB.
Test method used is Part 15 Subpart C 15.247 and overall test result is PASS.
The report was revised on 2025.05.19 to add the variant model and variant model information.
Summary of tests reports Pass for radiated restricted band and emission and Pass for antenna requirement.
The report states it was tested only for radiated restricted band and emission at the applicant's request and cannot be used for certification.

evidence.claim: The EUT frequency range is 2402 MHz to 2480 MHz in LE 1 Mbps mode.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequency range 2 402 ㎒ ~ 2 480 ㎒ (LE 1 Mbps)

evidence.claim: The EUT uses GFSK modulation, 40 channels, chip antenna with peak gain 3.5 dBi, and DC 29.6 V battery power.
evidence.evidenceText: Modulation technique GFSK; Number of channels 2 402 ㎒ ~ 2 480 ㎒ : 40 ch; Antenna specification Chip Antenna // Peak gain: 3.5 ㏈i; Power source DC 29.6 V (Battery)

evidence.claim: The EUT hardware version is Rev.0.0 and software version is 1.0.0.
evidence.evidenceText: H/W version Rev.0.0; S/W version 1.0.0

evidence.claim: The EUT must comply with FCC Part 15.247, KDB 558074 D01 v05 r02, and ANSI C63.10-2013.
evidence.evidenceText: the EUT must comply with following standards and KDB documents. FCC Part 15.247; KDB 558074 D01 v05 r02; ANSI C63.10-2013

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB variant differs only by appearance design from HnX-P1, while interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, and other items are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) is only changed the appearance design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: Radiated restricted band and emission testing under FCC 15.205 and 15.209 passed.
evidence.evidenceText: 15.205, 15.209 Radiated restricted band and emission Pass

evidence.claim: Antenna Requirement testing under FCC 15.203 passed.
evidence.evidenceText: 15.203 Antenna Requirement Pass

evidence.claim: Several FCC Part 15.247 conducted or transmitter tests were not tested because the device is equipped with an approved module.
evidence.evidenceText: 15.247(a)(2) 6 ㏈ bandwidth N/T; 15.247(b)(3) Output power N/T; 15.247(e) Power spectral density N/T; 15.247(d) Conducted spurious emission and band edge N/T; This device is exempt from the test because it is equipped with an approved module. Module report No. : ETLT180307.0036

evidence.claim: AC conducted emissions under FCC 15.207(a) were not tested at the applicant's request.
evidence.evidenceText: 15.207(a) AC Conducted emissions N/T; Not tested at applicant's request.

evidence.claim: The report cannot be used for certification.
evidence.evidenceText: This report was tested only for Radiated restricted band and emission at the request of the applicant and cannot be used for certification.

evidence.claim: No spurious emission was detected above 3 GHz for channels 00, 19, and 39.
evidence.evidenceText: No spurious emission were detected above 3 ㎓.

evidence.claim: No spurious emission was detected above 18 GHz in the worst case.
evidence.evidenceText: Test results (18 ㎓ to 30 ㎓) – Worst case ... No spurious emission were detected above 18 ㎓.

evidence.claim: KES states measurement equipment calibrations are traceable to National Primary Standards and SI units.
evidence.evidenceText: Statement of Traceability: KES Co., Ltd. attests that all calibrations have been performed, traceable to National Primary Standards and International System of Units (SI).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but source text identifies an FCC test report and FCC rules; FDA authority is not evidenced in the source text.
The report explicitly states it was tested only for radiated restricted band and emission at the applicant's request and cannot be used for certification.
Antenna Requirement is listed as FCC 15.203 in the summary, but section 3.2 text begins 'According to 15.207(a),' creating conflicting rule-section evidence.
The document date is inferred from the affirmation date and revision history date of 2025.05.19; no separate issue date field is provided.
